data.title: Tesla said to have delayed U.S. production of cheaper Model Y
data.description:
Electric vehicle maker Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) plans for an affordable U.S.-made version of its best-selling Model Y SUV has been delayed by at least a few months, Reuters reported on Friday, citing three sources with knowledge of the matter.

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) has publicly said it is on track to launch more affordable cars in H1 2025. However, according to the sources, global production of the cheaper Model Y in the U.S. will now start at least a few months later than the public plan. The sources offered several revised targets ranging from Q3 to early next year. 

According to Reuters, two of the sources said the company is aiming to produce 250K of the cheaper Model Ys in the U.S. in 2026. 

Tesla (TSLA) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment. 

The Reuters report comes ahead of the electric vehicle maker's Q1 results on April 22. Tesla (TSLA) has had a tough 2025, struggling with shrinking sales due to intense competition, and a wave of backlash against its products triggered by ire at top boss Elon Musk and his actions as a key government advisor to U.S. President Donald Trump.

TSLA stock is -40.2% YTD.

data.title: Fed proposes averaging bank stress test results over two years to reduce volatility
data.description:
The Federal Reserve on Thursday said it had proposed to average major banks' stress test results over two consecutive years to reduce the Y/Y changes in capital requirements.

The proposal is the first in a series of actions by the central bank to implement a major overhaul to its annual evaluation of a lender's ability to weather crises such as a financial crash or a recession. The stress test estimates a bank's losses, revenue and capital levels under such hypothetical severe scenarios.     

"Due to the changing hypothetical nature of the test, the results also change and introduce volatility each year. The results, in part, determine the calibration of the stress capital buffer (SCB), which is one component of the amount of capital large banks must hold to absorb losses," the Fed said in a statement.

The Fed's proposal would also give banks three additional months to adjust their new capital requirements by delaying the annual effective date of the SCB requirement from October 1 to January 1 of the following year. 

Finally, the proposal would make targeted changes to streamline stress test-related data collection. 

"These proposed changes are not designed to materially affect overall capital requirements," the Fed said.

The central bank in December last year unveiled its plans to overhaul the stress tests.

The Fed's announcement was immediately followed by a lawsuit on behalf of a group of major banks representing firms such as JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM), Citi (NYSE:C) and Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS). The group claimed that the stress tests continued to produce capital charges that were "inaccurate, volatile and excessive, resulting in reduced lending and economic growth."

As per Fed data, here are the top 10 largest commercial U.S. banks ranked by consolidated assets as of December 31, 2024: 

  1. JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM); consol. assets: $3.46T, SA Quant Rating: Hold.
  2. Bank of America (NYSE:BAC); consol. assets: $2.59T, SA Quant Rating: Hold.
  3. Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC); consol. assets: $1.71T, SA Quant Rating: Strong Buy.
  4. Citi (NYSE:C); consol. assets: $1.70T, SA Quant Rating: Hold.
  5. U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB); consol. assets: $662.91B, SA Quant Rating: Hold.
  6. Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS); consol. assets: $558.24B, SA Quant Rating: Hold.
  7. PNC Financial Services (NYSE:PNC); consol. assets: $556.14B, SA Quant Rating: Hold.
  8. Truist Financial (NYSE:TFC); consol. assets: $523.13B, SA Quant Rating: Hold. 
  9. Capital One Financial (NYSE:COF); consol. assets: $487.19B, SA Quant Rating: Strong Buy. 
  10. TD Bank (NYSE:TD); consol. assets: $372.78B, SA Quant Rating: Hold.

data.title: U.S. government COVID website replaced with China 'lab leak' theory
data.description:
The U.S. federal government's "covid.gov" website now redirects to a White House page offering up the unproven theory that the COVID-19 virus originated and was leaked from a Chinese laboratory.

The website earlier used to house information on the COVID-19 pandemic including facts on vaccines, treatment and testing. It was an official website of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services.

The website now takes the user to a "whitehouse.gov" address featuring a full-length image of U.S. President Donald Trump at the top along with the words "Lab Leak: The True Origins of COVID-19."

The website takes aim at the pandemic policies instituted by former President Joe Biden, and also criticized Biden's former chief medical officer Anthony Fauci for pushing what it called the "preferred narrative that COVID-19 originated naturally."

The "lab leak" theory was offered up by a U.S. House of Representatives subcommittee led by Rep. Brad Wenstrup (R-OH) in December 2024. 

The revamp of the COVID website comes amid numerous such changes and edits made to federal government web pages since Trump took office, reflecting the agenda of his administration.

Here are some exchange-traded funds tracking the Health Care sector: (NYSEARCA:XLV), (VHT), (IHI), (IXJ), (IYH), (FHLC), and (FXH).

And here are some stocks of COVID-19 vaccine makers: Moderna (MRNA), Pfizer (PFE), BioNTech (BNTX), Novavax (NVAX), Valneva (VALN), AstraZeneca (AZN) and Johnson & Johnson (JNJ).   

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Ford said to have halted shipments of F-150s and other models to China
data.description:
U.S. carmaker Ford (NYSE:F) has stopped shipments of some of its vehicle models, including its best-selling F-150 pickup truck, to China in order to avoid tariffs, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Ford (NYSE:F) halted shipments this week of F-150 Raptors, Mustang muscle cars and Bronco SUVs built in Mich., as well as Lincoln Navigators made in Ky., WSJ said, citing the people. 

According to the people, the U.S. and China's retaliatory trade barriers against each other have resulted in tariffs on those vehicles rising as high as 150%. The F-150 Raptor, for example, sells for nearly $100K in China.

Ford (F) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment. 

Two days ago, Automotive News reported that Ford (F) had told its dealers it might have to increase prices for consumers next month to offset the cost of tariffs. 

Of the major "Detroit Three" carmakers, Ford (F) has the least exposure to U.S. trade policy, with more cars made in the U.S. than rivals General Motors (GM) and Stellantis (STLA).

data.title: Capital One and Discover Financial's $35B merger gets final regulatory nods
data.description:
Capital One Financial (NYSE:COF) and Discover Financial Services (NYSE:DFS) on Friday said they had got the final regulatory approvals for their $35B merger, paving the way for what would become the eight-largest bank in the U.S.

The Federal Reserve Board and the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) approved the deal. 

"The OCC conducted a fulsome review of the application submitted March 21, 2024, to ensure all statutory and regulatory requirements have been met," the agency said in a statement. "Today’s announcement reflects the OCC’s careful analysis of the effect of the merger on communities, the banking industry, and the U.S. financial system," it added. 

The Fed Board also entered into a consent order with Discover Financial (NYSE:DFS) and assessed the lender a fine of $100M for "overcharging certain interchange fees from 2007 through 2023." DFS has "since terminated these practices and is repaying those fees to affected customers," the Board said in a statement.

According to the Fed, the combined entity post the merger would become the eight largest U.S. insured depository organization, with consolidated assets of around $637.8B. That figure would represent 2.2% of the country's total insured deposits.

"There will be no immediate changes to Capital One and Discover customer accounts and relationships now or in the period immediately following the closing of the transaction," the banks said in a joint statement.

The merger is expected to close on May 18.

data.title: Eli Lilly plans to make weight-loss pill in U.S. amid Trump’s reshoring push
data.description:
Eli Lilly & Co. (LLY) announced Friday that it plans to manufacture its experimental weight-loss pill in the U.S. as President Trump emphasizes reshoring manufacturing to the U.S.

“We’re going to make this medicine here in the United States,” Chief Executive Officer Dave Ricks said Friday in an interview with Fox Business Network’s Maria Bartiromo. “We’ll be proud to make this medicine at that site and ship it around the world from our country.”

Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) shares closed 14% higher yesterday after the company said its oral GLP-1 receptor agonist orforglipron reached the main goal in a Phase 3 trial for adults with type 2 diabetes.

The Indiana-based pharma giant said that the once-daily pill reduced weight by ~8% on average (16.0 lbs) at the highest dose in the 40-week trial.

Eli Lilly said in February that it will invest at least $27 billion to build four new manufacturing sites in the U.S. as demand for its weight loss and diabetes injections soars and the company develops new drugs. The announcement brings the company's total U.S. manufacturing investments to more than $50 billion in recent years.

The move comes as drugmakers and companies across different industries work to build goodwill with President Donald Trump, who has emphasized reshoring manufacturing to the U.S. and reducing reliance on foreign supply chains.

data.title: Most and least shorted S&P 500 consumer discretionary stocks in March
data.description:
Average short interest across the S&P 500 consumer discretionary stocks has increased to 2.69% of shares float in end-March, from 2.64% a month earlier.

Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) has been up 8.26% over the year, as compared to the 5.42% rise in the S&P500 index over the same period.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

 

Caesars Entertainment (CZR) was the most shorted stock at 11.87% of shares float, followed by Tapestry (TPR) with short interest of 10.42% and Pool (POOL) with 8.35%, respectively. 

Amazon.com (AMZN) was the least shorted stock, with 70.72M shares sold short or 0.75% of the shares float, followed by Home Depot (HD) and TJX (TJX) with short interest of 0.96% and 1.21%, respectively.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

 

Textiles, Apparel & Luxury Goods was the most shorted industry within the sector, with 4.46% short interest as of the end of March, marginally down from 4.73% at the end of February.

Automobile Components stocks took the second spot with 4.23% short interest as of March end, compared to 4.37% a month ago.

Leisure Equipment & Products sector took the third spot with short interest of 4.04% at the end of March, compared to 4.11% at February-end. 

What Quantitative Measures say

Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system gives Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) a Hold rating, with 2.78 score.

The ETF has a strong liquidity grade of A+ and momentum grade of also C+. Notably, the ratings' system sees risk as a threat and assigns the ETF a grade of D in this category.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Spotlight on Tesla, Alphabet earnings, AACR drug data, and global PMI reports
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - April 21

  • Volatility watch - Nvidia (NVDA) has a much higher level of implied volatility than normal based on options trading. Companies with an elevated level of short interest outstanding on them ahead of earnings include Lemonade (LMND), Gogo (GOGO), and CleanSpark (CLSK). The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Janover (JNVR), Nutex Health (NUTX), and ELong Power Holding (ELPW). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Destination XL (DXLG), SunCar (SDA) and Ironwood (IRWD).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Comerica (CMA), WR Berkley (WRB) and Medpace Holdings (MEDP).

 

Tuesday - April 22

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Tesla (TSLA), GE Aerospace (GE), Verizon (VZ), Lockheed Martin (LMT), Northrop Grumman (NOC), Capital One (COF), and 3M (MMM). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Baker Hughes (BKR) and Equifax (EFX) after their reports are released.
  • All day - The IMF will release its full World Economic Outlook and Global Financial Stability reports.
  • 9:00 a.m. Philadelphia Federal Reserve Bank President Patrick Harker will give welcome remarks before the Economic Mobility Summit sponsored by the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia.
  • 2:00 p.m. Minneapolis Federal Reserve Bank President Neel Kashkari will participate in a moderated question-and-answer session before the U.S. Chamber of Commerce Global Summit.

 

Wednesday - April 23

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Philip Morris International (PM), IBM (IBM), AT&T (T), Texas Instruments (TXN), Boeing (BA), and Chipotle (CMG). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for QuantumScape (QS) and IMAX (IMAX) after their reports are released.
  • All day - Goldman Sachs (GS) will hold its annual general meeting. Proxy adviser firm Glass Lewis has recommended that shareholders reject Goldman's executive retention bonuses that total $160 million.
  • All day - Global flash PMI reports will be the focus of economists in an early gauge of the impact of the trade war.
  • All day - The Shanghai International Show Auto will feature press events and model introductions from major auto brands, including Lexus (TM), Audi, Volkswagen (OTCPK:VLKAF), Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGAF), NIO (NIO), BYD Company (OTCPK:BYDDF), Zeekr Intelligent (ZK), Li Auto (LI), and XPeng (XPEV). Notably, Xiaomo (OTCPK:XIACF) will make its first time appearance at the show, while Bosch, CATL, Sony (SONY), and Intel (INTC) will be first-time exhibitors in the supply chain and tech halls.
  • All day - The three-day Semafor World Economy Summit will begin. Notable speakers include General Motors (GM) CEO Mary Barra, Pfizer (PFE) CEO Albert Bourla, Philip Morris International (PM) CEO Jacek Olczak, and PayPal (PYPL) CEO Alex Chriss.
  • All day -  Autolus Therapeutics (AUTL) will host an R&D investor event. The company will present an update on clinical pipeline programs, including plans to expand the obe-cel opportunity.
  • 9:00 a.m. Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee will speak virtually before the Economic Mobility Summit sponsored by the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.
  • 6:30 p.m. Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack will speak before the Money Marketeers of New York University.

 

Thursday - April 24

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Alphabet (GOOG), Procter & Gamble (PG), T-Mobile US (TMUS), Merck (MRK), PepsiCo (PEP), American Airlines (AAL), Gilead Sciences (GILD), Comcast (CMCSA), and Intel (INTC). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Skechers (SKX) and Hasbro (HAS) after their reports are released 
  • All day - MGM Resorts International (MGM) will hold an event to commemorate the groundbreaking of its integrated casino resort in Osaka, Japan.
  • 5:00 p.m. Minneapolis Federal Reserve Bank President Neel Kashkari will participate in a moderated question-and-answer session before the University of Minnesota College of Science and Engineering event, "Innovating the Future: Perspectives on Science, Engineering and the Economy."

 

Friday - April 25

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Centene (CNC), Abbvie (ABBV), Colgate-Palmolive (CL), Charter (CHTR), and AutoNation (AN).
  • All day - The six-day American Association of Cancer Research annual meeting will begin. The AACR meeting serves as a critical platform for unveiling the latest scientific breakthroughs, clinical trial data, and technological innovations in cancer research and treatment, particularly for biotech and pharma companies presenting pivotal clinical trial data or announcing major R&D advances. Companies participating include Amgen (AMGN), AstraZeneca (AZN), Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY), Eli Lilly & Company (LLY), Gilead Sciences (GILD), Merck (MRK), Novartis (NVS), and Pfizer (PFE).
  • All day - Abbott Laboratories (ABT) will hold its annual general meeting.
  • 4:30 p.m. The Nasdaq short interest report will be released.

data.title: Cognizant expands partnership with Docusign
data.description:
• Cognizant (NASDAQ:CTSH) said on Friday that it has expanded its partnership with Docusign (DOCU) to enhance customer support and drive digital transformation. 
  • This collaboration expands the existing 360-degree relationship with Cognizant as a services provider, go-to-market partner and customer, deepening the strategic partnership.
  • Cognizant and Docusign are working together to provide innovative intelligent agreement management (IAM) solutions that optimize customer service management and streamline agreement processes globally.
  • As part of a multi-year agreement, Cognizant will provide comprehensive customer support services for Docusign, including assistance with eSignature, billing inquiries and technical support.
